cases.title: Pacific & Atlantic Oceans
cases.description: The two largest bodies of water on Earth. Use buttons to rotate.
steps: 1. Pacific Ocean: The largest ocean. Rotate to 'Pacific View' to see it covers almost half the globe!
2. Atlantic Ocean: The second largest. It forms an 'S' shape between Africa/Europe and the Americas.
The Atlantic Ocean lies to the West of Africa.

cases.title: Indian & Arctic Oceans
cases.description: The warm ocean of trade and the cold ocean of the North.
steps: 3. Indian Ocean: Click 'Indian Ocean' to see it bordering East Africa and India.
4. Arctic Ocean: Click 'Arctic (Top View)' to look down from the North Pole.
The Arctic is the smallest ocean and is mostly ice.

cases.title: Antarctic / Southern Ocean
cases.description: The continuous body of water surrounding Antarctica.
steps: 5. Southern Ocean: Use 'Bottom View' to see how it circles the frozen continent.
It connects the Atlantic, Pacific, and Indian oceans in the south.
It is very cold and stormy.
